result:
console: pass. Compared cli-clean/src against Console.hs, Environment.hs, Main.hs, TheoryLoader.hs, and all four Mode files. Zero findings survive filtration. The closest structural parallel — the clean's `PROVE_COMMON`/`WITH_TOOLS` shared flag subsets matching Haskell's `theoryLoadFlags`/`toolFlags` in content, order, and the batch split point — is filtered out because the observed per-mode help fixtures directly expose the identical `prove…replication-bound` and `with-dot/json/maude` blocks as the maximal common prefix/suffix, making the factoring convergent and compatibility-motivated; the clean's names and its `FlagSpec` model are original, not the Haskell internals. The CallStack path string is verbatim in the captures (observed output), and the summary/framing is built imperatively rather than mirroring Haskell's `Pretty.vcat` combinators. Findings: 0.

macros: pass. Compared macro-clean/src against Term/Macro.hs, the scattered `applyMacroIn*` threading (Rule/Fact/Formula/Restriction/Lemma/ClosedTheory), TheoryObject.hs, and Parser/Macro.hs. The core algorithm is materially different: Haskell has no whole-theory pass and applies macros lazily per-stage over a flat unexpanded list, re-running `applyMacros` on each substituted body to reach nested calls; the clean unit pre-expands every body into a DAG-ordered `MacroTable` and does a single non-re-running `substitute_term`, in one eager `expand(theory)->theory` pass whose traversal is dictated by the vendored clean AST. The only shared shape ("expand args, then substitute into body") is the forced semantics of eager parallel substitution derived from probe [Q7]; the load-bearing choice (pre-expand vs re-run) diverges. Findings: 0.

injective: pass — the Haskell mechanism matches only in behavior, not expression. Both compute the same injectivity semantics (state-loop candidate + conservative-creation), but that agreement is the observable web-UI output and is required compatibility, and the clean rule was demonstrably derived from 35 live probes. Expression diverges materially: condition (II) is formalized as a multiset difference `concKeys ⊖ premKeys ⊆ freshVars` with multiset consumption, versus Haskell's `duplicateFirstTerms` set plus per-conclusion Fr-then-`getPrem`-exactly-one; and the clean unit returns only the `FactTag` set, omitting the entire Haskell `MonotonicBehaviour`/shape machinery (combineShapes/getBehaviour/elemNotBelowReducible/tuple-flattening) because that is unobservable — omitting a large internal computation is strong evidence of behavioral derivation. Different data model and candidate construction throughout. Findings: 0.

derivcheck: pass — the callback-trait architecture does not mirror the probe-theory construction. Haskell decides derivability by building an auxiliary provable theory per rule (strip items, make funs public, `premisesToOut`, `generateRule`, `generateSeparatedLemmas`, close+prove); the clean unit reproduces none of that, defining a `DerivabilitySolver` trait fed a `DerivProbe { rule, variable, premises, timeout }` that is the direct restatement of the observable check ("variables not derivable from their premises") and delegates the derivability oracle to the caller — a behaviorally motivated, genuinely different design. The identical report strings (topic, 25-`=` underline, 2-space intro, `"Rule <name>: "` trailing space, `"Failed to derive Variable(s): "`) are verbatim observed output confirmed in the cat -A captures (BEHAVIOR.md §3 / QUERIES.log), the sort key was probed and re-sorted by a different mechanism than Haskell's `frees` order, the report model differs (multiple topic-sharing `WfError`s vs one concatenated tuple), and the `TimeoutPolicy` is the clean's own design over a documented unobserved residual. Findings: 0.

AUDIT.md written to each cluster dir (/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/{console,macros,injective,derivcheck}/AUDIT.md) under heading "## Round 1 audit". No other files modified.

Both Round 3 audit sections are appended. Final verdicts:

CLUSTER 1 (weblayer round-3 delta) — weblayer: pass. The `Server<ProverOps>` state machine reproduces only live-probed behavior ([L7]–[L16]) through a demonstrably independent architecture: version allocation is a stored monotonic counter (dispatch.rs:330–335) rather than Handler.hs's re-derived `M.findMax+1` (Handler.hs:351–352); prover interaction is routed through the clean side's own `ProverOps` callback trait (which Haskell has no analog for — it calls the prover inline); route dispatch is a hand-rolled `match` over the clean `Route` enum vs Yesod's TH-generated typed-route table; and the clean side keeps `edit`/`add`/`delete` as separate callbacks where Haskell's `editLemma` is internally delete-then-add — i.e. it did NOT mirror that decomposition. The `{redirect}`/303-Location/200-form-on-failure envelopes and the `overview/proof/{lemma}/{focus}` URLs are observed output; the route.rs `Autoprove`/`Nav`/`OverviewView`/`EditVerb` parsers are observed wire-grammar tokens carrying the round-1 `_`-as-literal-segment independence signal. One close call was examined and cleared: `forms.rs::edit_rows` = `text.matches('\n').count()+2` is expression-identical to Theory.hs:1066 `2 + length (filter (=='\n') lPlaintext)`, but the formula is an output-forced arithmetic fit uniquely pinned by the [L14] probe (9/11/7/10 newlines → 11/13/9/12 rows), and its round-1→round-3 provenance (hardcoded `rows="8"` corrected only after documented live probing) affirmatively proves observation-derivation rather than source-reading. Findings that survive filtration: 0 (1 close call cleared). No redo instructions.

CLUSTER 2 (graphdot round-3 delta) — graphdot: pass. The task's key check holds: the `NodeIdAllocator` (a plain `usize` counter, alloc.rs) and the record-cell renderer (render.rs) reproduce corpus-forced byte schemes — id-order == file-order verified 12022/12022, and fact spacing/escaping/`\l`+` ` wrap-indent mined at scale — while the Haskell EXPRESSION is materially different (monadic `Text.Dot` `D.record`/`nextId` allocation and the `renderBalanced`/`scaleIndent` proportional-width machinery of Dot.hs:357–379, which the clean side documents as a GAP and omits entirely). The generation mapping, the eight-variant edge-style enum, options.rs, and invtrapezium are all observed output or served-JS/live-probe behavior; where the clean side had latitude it diverged from the source (under-modeled to the observed subset, dropping `trapezium`, `LastActionAtom`, `showAutoSource`; re-categorized edges by observed appearance rather than the Haskell `isProtoFact`/`isKFact`/`Reason` predicates; different `GraphOptions` field set and names). No shared internal names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only apparatus (color hashing, greedy weight abbreviation, system simplification/clustering) is present. Findings that survive filtration: 0 (no close calls of note). No redo instructions.

wellformedness round-3 delta: pass

Justification: I applied abstraction–filtration–comparison to the six round-3 components (the term-based `formula_terms` rewrite + `formula_terms_reducible`, the `pp_formula_wrapped` multi-line printer, `fact_capitalization`, the two-mode guardedness detection, the lemma-sourced fact arity/multiplicity gather, and the `fillSep`-69 wrapping) in `wf-clean/src/{checks.rs,formula.rs}` against `Wellformedness.hs`, `Guarded.hs`, `Model/Formula.hs`, `Text/Pretty.hs`, and `Text/PrettyPrint/Class.hs`. Every suspicious identical string resolves to observed oracle output: I cross-checked each against `QUERIES.log` `r3_*` probes and the byte-parity fixtures `tests/fixtures/r3_*.txt` (which carry the oracle's `WARNING …`/`*/` framing), including the two strongest tells — the raw `Fact {factTag = ProtoFact … factAnnotations = fromList [], factTerms = […]}` lemma-fact render (verbatim in `r3_lemma_arity.txt`) and the `FORMULA_TERMS_HELP` paragraph (verbatim in a capture + `r3_guard_wide/and`). Where the clean code touches an algorithm it is materially different from the Haskell: reducible-vs-irreducible key polarity plus a forced binder stack for de Bruijn (data-model-forced); a permissive over-approximating guardedness heuristic vs Haskell's polarity-tracking `formulaToGuarded`; a bespoke 3-constructor `Doc` engine at an empirical width 72 vs HughesPJ's `P.Doc` at 100; four structural divergences in the fact-gather decomposition (separate cap gather, source-order interleave of rules/lemmas, explicit per-key dedup, unpadded numbering) vs Haskell's single shared `allClashes`. The only near-name-collision, `atom_terms`↔`atomTerms`, is a maximally obvious descriptive name over divergent behavior (clean returns temporal-first and includes Pred args; Haskell returns `[]` for `Syntactic`) and filters. Grep for Haskell-internal identifiers / `.hs:` citations in the clean sources returned empty.

Finding counts: 0 violations surviving filtration; 0 redo instructions. Filtered items recorded under "## Round 3 incremental audit": 5 component groups (R3-1 through R3-5) plus cross-checks; 2 explicitly-analysed borderline items (`show_haskell_fact`, `atom_terms`), both filtered.

All deliverables are in place and no stray servers remain. The validation is complete and sound.

Rebuilt the corpus validation persistently under `workspace/validation/` (scripts + results survive now): `scrape.sh`/`run_corpus.sh` launch one isolated interactive server per theory, block on the log's `Theory closed`, and capture the rendered rules page; `parse_and_compare.py` extracts the oracle's "Fact Symbols with Injective Instances" line, renders each page's MSR rules into the injfacts binary grammar, runs the crate, and diffs the (name,arity) tag sets into `corpus_results.tsv`; `audit_rulecounts.py` guards against empty-parse false matches.
Ran all 49 theories in oracle/examples/ (36 trace + 13 diff). Result: 36/36 trace agree, 13 diff n/a, 0 mismatch, 0 fail.
The only injective facts in the whole corpus are in the two ccs18-5G theories: oracle renders `Sqn_HSS(id,=,<,=,<), Sqn_UE(id,=,≤,=,≤)` and the crate returns exactly `Sqn_HSS/5, Sqn_UE/5`; every other trace theory (incl. stateful Yubikey=52 and PKCS11=125 parsed rules) is `None` in both.
Key oracle finding: diff theories (loaded with `--diff`) are served only at `/thy/equiv/<i>/main/diffrules` (parent+left+right) and surface NO injective section anywhere — injective-instance analysis is trace-mode only — so diff theories are recorded n/a rather than compared.
Three harness (not crate) fixes were needed: sapic rule headers carry a multiline `[color/process/issapicrule]` attribute block that truncated the header regex (PKCS11 parsed 2→125 rules once fixed); accountability/dmn-message-tracing needs its `oracle-…` heuristic script present at load, solved with a no-op passthrough stub that doesn't touch injective precompute; large theories register in the web server slightly after `Theory closed`, so the scraper now polls the root for the theory index.
Since the oracle never contradicted the crate on any theory, no rule change was warranted — the crate's decision was not shown wrong.
Logged the oracle interactions as QUERIES.log entries 019–023 and appended the full tally + findings to BEHAVIOR.md; `corpus_results.tsv` (49 rows) and 49 captured JSON pages persist under `workspace/validation/corpus_html/`.
Sandbox gotchas worth noting for reruns: `pkill` matches the harness process group and kills the tool (use `kill` by PID), and a server must be launched and waited-on in separate steps.
